established: equivalence is assessed only between machinery-closed representation packages
positive equivalence requires effective total bidirectional correspondences
round-trip identity is required for facts, states, relations, queries, and machinery
structural, semantic, operational, dependency, informational, historical, query, error, identity, and machinery dimensions must all be resolved
behavioral or final-output equality alone is insufficient
dependency, history, identity, error/Unknown, and support-machinery distinctions cannot be erased by canonicalization
unresolved totality, effectivity, or dimensions yield Unknown absent positive failure
